taxonomy: Notoceras bicorne is classified as a genus of extinct nautiloid cephalopods, specifically within the family Notoceratidae. This classification places it within the larger group of cephalopods, a class of marine animals that also includes squid, octopuses, and other nautiluses. As a member of the class Cephalopoda, Notoceras bicorne shares characteristics such as a distinct shell and radial symmetry with other cephalopods. The family Notoceratidae is part of the superfamily Notoceratoidea, which contains other extinct nautiloid families. Further classification places Notoceras bicorne within the phylum Mollusca.
